message:
fix(e2e): busy-retry pragma setup; barrier-wait on early worker exit

Both fsqlite and sqlite executor workers now retry PRAGMA execution up
to 100 times (fsqlite) or max_busy_retries (sqlite) with backoff when
the database returns BUSY during connection setup. This prevents
spurious test failures in high-concurrency e2e scenarios where WAL
recovery or checkpoint holds the lock during worker initialization.

Also ensures workers call barrier.wait() before returning on open or
configure failures, preventing the barrier from hanging indefinitely
when other workers are still waiting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
